notes: Apply with Vercel Firewall API or recreate in the Vercel dashboard before inviting the first early-access cohort.
The first rule is the minimum viable rule for Hobby plans because Hobby allows one rate-limit rule.
Use projectId and teamId from .vercel/project.json or the Vercel dashboard. Do not commit VERCEL_TOKEN.

value.name: Memoire cohort - expensive AI endpoint rate limit
value.description: Protect AI and embedding endpoints beyond app-level in-memory limits before a controlled early-access cohort.

value.name: Memoire cohort - request access submission rate limit
value.description: Reduce spam on the lead-capture endpoint while preserving privacy-minimized product events.

value.name: Memoire cohort - challenge obvious scripted API clients
value.description: Challenge common scripted user agents on API paths while preserving verified browsers.

verification: Open Vercel project firewall events and confirm the rules are active.
Send a normal browser request through the demo and signed-in app; it should not be challenged.
Use a scripted client against one protected AI endpoint until the limit is exceeded; Vercel should return HTTP 429.
Confirm application-level rate limits still return app JSON 429s when user-level limits are exceeded.
Confirm Stripe webhook traffic is not rate-limited by these rules because /api/stripe-webhook is excluded.
